text.title: Cloud Architecture Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every application of `Cloud Architecture` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Design MUST be reviewed against all six pillars (operational excellence / security / reliability / performance / cost / sustainability). Skipping a pillar = INCOMPLETE.
rule.rationale: Skipped pillars become incidents under that pillar.

rule.statement: Each cloud choice (provider, region, network, identity, storage tier) MUST land in an ADR with rationale + ≥2 rejected alternatives.
rule.rationale: Implicit choices are unauditable and unreversible.

rule.statement: IaC artefacts MUST mirror the ADR — drift detection (terraform plan / pulumi preview) runs on every change.
rule.rationale: ADR-without-IaC is decoration; IaC-without-ADR is unauditable change.

rule.statement: DR strategy MUST state RTO + RPO per workload tier (T0/T1/T2). Default tier without targets = T2.
VPC CIDR ranges MUST be reserved in an IPAM register before account creation. Resizing CIDR post-creation is forbidden (re-create instead).
rule.rationale: AWS/Azure VPC CIDRs cannot resize — the lock-in window is account creation.

rule.statement: Every workload has a monthly budget + alert threshold; deploy is blocked until both are set.
rule.rationale: Unbudgeted cloud workloads are the leading SaaS-cost runaway pattern.

rule.statement: No-implicit-trust between workloads; service-to-service auth via mTLS or signed JWT, never network-trust.
rule.rationale: Implicit trust = lateral-movement vector under any compromise.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
